Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Innovative Corporation Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Innovative Corporation Pte Ltd was incorporated in Singapore on 16 August 2004.
Ms Annie Chen Liping was a director and major shareholder and acted as the main decision-maker for the company.
The company's business focus shifted to property development and building construction.
Tianjin Heping Construction Group Co Ltd and Fong Yun Thai Association signed a Cooperation Agreement for a residential project at 33 Holland Link.
Under the Cooperation Agreement, the project involved building 82 semi-detached units with an estimated construction cost of S$115 million.
Excavation works at the 33 Holland Link site commenced, carried out by a related construction entity.
A draft Joint Venture Agreement identified Innovative Corporation Pte Ltd as the developer appointed for the project.
Mr Ow Chun Ming proposed to become a 50% shareholder in Innovative Corporation Pte Ltd to jointly undertake the 33 Holland Link project.
Mr Ow Chun Ming was recorded in regulatory records as a director of Innovative Corporation Pte Ltd.
500,000 shares, representing 50% of the issued share capital, were transferred to Mr Ow Chun Ming.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Innovative Corporation Pte Ltd had a maturing business opportunity to act as the developer for the 33 Holland Link project, and whether the actions of its director-shareholder constituted a diversion of that opportunity.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors owe a fiduciary duty to act in the best interests of the company. This includes a strict prohibition against exploiting or diverting maturing corporate opportunities that the company is actively pursuing, regardless of whether the company could ultimately secure the opportunity.
Party_Details.Application: Innovative Corporation Pte Ltd invested resources into the 33 Holland Link project, including participating in site excavation and negotiating a draft Joint Venture Agreement naming it as the developer. Ow Chun Ming joined the company as a director and 50% shareholder to assist with the project. During his tenure, he participated in regulatory meetings where he was presented as the replacement developer and subsequently submitted a personal tender for the project through a separate vehicle without the company's informed consent.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The 33 Holland Link project represents a maturing corporate opportunity because the company had advanced beyond speculation into physical site work and detailed negotiations. A director's fiduciary duties prohibit the appropriation of such an opportunity. The rapid engagement with the project partner, participation in regulatory meetings as the new developer, and submission of a personal tender while still holding office constitute a clear breach of the duty of loyalty. Liability for the diversion is established independent of the company's ultimate capacity to complete the project.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Ow Chun Ming

Facts.Text: Ow Chun Ming is a real estate developer with 30 years of experience and Chairman of the Clydesbuilt Group.
Ow Chun Ming observed excavation works at the project site and engaged with the plaintiff company regarding the Joint Venture Agreement.
Ow Chun Ming was formally registered as a director and 50% shareholder of Innovative Corporation Pte Ltd.
Ow Chun Ming attended a meeting with the Urban Redevelopment Authority seeking an extension for the project, where he was suggested as the new developer.
Ow Chun Ming submitted a personal bid for the project while still listed as a director of the plaintiff company.
The board of the land-owning association accepted Ow Chun Ming's personal proposal for the project.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Ow Chun Ming’s role as a director and 50% shareholder of Innovative Corporation Pte Ltd created a fiduciary conflict when he personally pursued the 33 Holland Link project.
Party_Details.Rule: A director must utilize project information and counterpart relationships obtained in their corporate capacity solely for the company’s benefit. A director cannot compete with the company by pursuing the same defined opportunity through separate vehicles while holding office.
Party_Details.Application: Ow Chun Ming acquired detailed knowledge of the 33 Holland Link project through the plaintiff company and became a director and 50% shareholder to help realize the development. However, he attended regulatory meetings with the project partner where he was introduced as a replacement developer. He received a formal invitation to tender and submitted a personal bid before his formal resignation as a director was recorded, subsequently incorporating a new vehicle to carry out the development.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The submission of a personal bid for a project that the company is actively pursuing presents a direct conflict of interest. Fiduciary duties apply strictly to existing, maturing opportunities. The director's presentation of a new development team to regulators and his tender submission while holding office represent the unauthorized appropriation of a corporate opportunity. The absence of informed consent or shareholder ratification confirms the breach of fiduciary duty.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Clydesbuilt (Holland Link) Pte. Ltd.

Facts.Text: Clydesbuilt (Holland Link) Pte. Ltd. was incorporated as a special-purpose vehicle for the purpose of carrying out the 33 Holland Link residential project.
Ow Chun Ming served as the controlling director and mind of the company upon its incorporation.
The company entered into a joint venture agreement with the land-owning association to build the residential units.
The company acquired the project land for S$70 million via a set-off arrangement against development costs.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Clydesbuilt (Holland Link) Pte. Ltd. is liable for knowing receipt and dishonest assistance upon receiving the 33 Holland Link project diverted by its controlling director.
Party_Details.Rule: A corporate entity is liable for knowing receipt if it receives a business opportunity resulting from a breach of fiduciary duty with knowledge of that breach. It is liable for dishonest assistance if it participates in the breach with a level of knowledge making its participation unconscionable. The knowledge of the controlling mind is imputed to the entity.
Party_Details.Application: Clydesbuilt (Holland Link) Pte. Ltd. was incorporated specifically to execute the 33 Holland Link development project, an opportunity diverted from a competing firm. Ow Chun Ming, acting as the company's controlling mind, had full knowledge that the opportunity was obtained through his own conflict of interest while serving as a director of the competing firm. The company received the benefit of the land acquisition and the development profits traceable to this conduct.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The entity's knowledge is identical to that of its controlling director. Since the director knew the acquisition of the project stemmed from a breach of his fiduciary duties to a former company, that knowledge is imputed directly to the vehicle. The receipt of the project opportunity and subsequent profits constitutes knowing receipt of assets traceable to a breach of trust. Furthermore, active participation in the joint venture to realize the opportunity constitutes dishonest assistance.
